title: Happy Ending

jokes.text: What happened? Who died? - Who died? Did Patrick die? No, Patrick didn't die, David. Then why did you startle me awake like this? My mind went straight to some kind of unthinkable tragedy.
jokes.setup: David being woken up and immediately assuming the worst
jokes.punchline: His catastrophic thinking specifically jumping to Patrick dying from being woken up
jokes.explanation: Perfect David Rose neurotic escalation - his anxious mind immediately jumps from being woken up to his fiancé dying. Shows his character's catastrophic thinking pattern beautifully.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? What happened? Is Patrick dead?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, he's fine. I just wanted to tell you something.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, you can't just wake someone up like that. You could kill them.

jokes.text: Well, the day did have a slight singultus - What is she talking about? - A hiccup.
jokes.setup: Moira using an unnecessarily fancy word for a simple concept
jokes.punchline: David's immediate translation showing he's used to this behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary pretension with David's practiced translation. Shows their established dynamic perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm feeling rather pellucid today.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're transparent.

jokes.text: Oh my God. To where? Bob's Garage? No, his roof was no match for the cumulus clouds.
jokes.setup: David sarcastically suggesting Bob's Garage as a venue
jokes.punchline: Moira's overly dramatic meteorological explanation for why even that won't work
jokes.explanation: David's sarcastic desperation paired with Moira's unnecessarily poetic weather reporting creates good character contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, Bob's Garage is located in a low-lying basin, which means it experiences its own microclimate. During the spring equinox, there's a convergence of warm and cold fronts that creates a perpetual mist. It's essentially a swamp.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's a parking lot.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A swamp, David.

jokes.text: I've woken up in a 'Black Mirror' episode.
jokes.setup: David processing the wedding disaster
jokes.punchline: Reference to dystopian TV show captures his sense of reality breaking down
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference that perfectly captures David's dramatic worldview. Economy is excellent - captures his mental state in four words.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's like The Handmaid's Tale, except everyone's fertile and no one's being punished.

jokes.text: What kind of adult man rides a penny-farthing? He's a haikuist!
jokes.setup: David questioning the officiant's transportation choice
jokes.punchline: Moira's explanation that being a haikuist somehow justifies this
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd logic - Moira treats being a poetry writer as sufficient explanation for riding an antique bicycle. The non-sequitur reasoning is peak Moira.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're a wedding officiant arriving in a hot air balloon?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm also a haikuist, David.

jokes.text: You can barely get through this without crying, so that's a no.
jokes.setup: Johnny offering to officiate the wedding
jokes.punchline: David immediately shutting him down for being too emotional
jokes.explanation: David's practical assessment of his father's emotional state. Shows family dynamics and David's need for control on his wedding day.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, I would be honored to officiate your wedding.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

jokes.text: A note, I did bring the house down at Pat Sajak's first wedding. 'Can I buy a vow.' I had the studio audience wrapped around my finger.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending his ability to officiate by citing past performance
jokes.punchline: Wheel of Fortune pun at a celebrity wedding - absurdly specific boast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific callback to Johnny's entertainment industry past with a clever Wheel of Fortune wordplay. The absurd specificity makes it memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I've officiated seventeen weddings. Seventeen! And do you know how many of those couples are still together? Well, I don't know the exact number, but I'm quite certain it's more than zero. Plus, I once correctly identified a Wheel of Fortune puzzle at a celebrity wedding before the bride and groom did. It was 'LOVE IS IN THE AIR.' I solved it in my head. So clearly, I have a gift for this sort of thing.

jokes.text: We have money now and the last thing I want is to leave town with some cheap-ass, soggy wedding.
jokes.setup: Family discussing wedding solutions
jokes.punchline: Alexis's blunt description combining the family's new wealth with the practical problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Alexis showing her direct nature and family loyalty. 'Cheap-ass, soggy' is funny phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, we have money now, so we can just buy a new wedding.

jokes.text: Fuck! Fuck! Fuck! Fuck! - Yep. - Mhmm. Where are you going?! Somewhere safe.
jokes.setup: David having a complete meltdown
jokes.punchline: Everyone's casual acceptance of his breakdown and his need to flee to safety
jokes.explanation: Classic David stress response with everyone matter-of-factly accepting his breakdown. The calm reactions make his panic funnier.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I can't do this anymore! I just— I can't!

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: It's okay, it's okay.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, babe, you're fine.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, you don't understand. I need to leave. Right now.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, go ahead.

jokes.text: Wow, let's not get crazy. No, we should have gotten married indoors. You said indoor weddings are tacky. No. I said most weddings are tacky. And they often take place indoors.
jokes.setup: David admitting they should have had an indoor wedding
jokes.punchline: His convoluted logic to avoid admitting he was wrong about indoor weddings
jokes.explanation: Perfect David logic - he can't admit he was wrong so he creates a distinction that technically makes him still right. Shows his need to maintain superiority even in crisis.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Look, I never said an indoor wedding was a bad idea. I said that outdoor weddings were better for the aesthetic, which they are. But that doesn't mean indoor weddings are bad. They're just... different. And different isn't worse, it's just different.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: So you're saying...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm saying that if we had done it indoors, we would have had an indoor wedding, which would have been fine. But we did it outdoors, and it was also fine. So really, both scenarios were equally fine, which means I was right either way.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: That's not how being right works.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it is now.

jokes.text: Besides those carpets do not photograph well.
jokes.setup: David explaining why Roland's living room won't work
jokes.punchline: His superficial concern about carpet aesthetics in photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect David concern - focusing on photo aesthetics rather than practical issues. Shows his character priorities clearly.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The carpet is a sort of... burnt sienna, which photographs terribly. And in the context of these photos, it's going to read as just a sort of... depressing rust.

jokes.text: Oh no, all this was planned weeks ago. Just had a hunch you wouldn't handle stress well today.
jokes.setup: David asking how Patrick booked a massage last-minute
jokes.punchline: Patrick's prescient understanding of David's stress patterns
jokes.explanation: Excellent relationship humor showing Patrick knows David perfectly. Great setup and punchline structure.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, I know you. By next Tuesday, you're going to be wound so tight that you'll need a deep tissue massage just to remember what relaxation feels like.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's fair.

jokes.text: How did you book a massage so last-minute? Oh no, all this was planned weeks ago. Just had a hunch you wouldn't handle stress well today.
jokes.setup: David wondering how Patrick arranged last-minute stress relief
jokes.punchline: Patrick revealing he planned for David's inevitable breakdown weeks in advance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Patrick knows David so well he planned for his wedding day meltdown. Sweet and funny relationship dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How did you even manage to pull this together on such short notice?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't. I planned this weeks ago. I know you, David. I knew you'd have a breakdown eventually, so I just... prepared for it.

jokes.text: You had like three bagels in half an hour. - They were a mistake.
jokes.setup: Patrick providing evidence of David's stress eating
jokes.punchline: David's weak defense of his bagel consumption as unintentional
jokes.explanation: Relatable stress-eating joke with David's ridiculous justification. The word 'mistake' for eating bagels is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You've eaten five bagels in the last two days.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I didn't intend to.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You didn't intend to eat five bagels?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: 'Hello, you. Are-are we all ready to spin the wheel of life today?' Even Vanna White didn't laugh at that one.
jokes.setup: Johnny practicing wedding jokes with Wheel of Fortune references
jokes.punchline: His self-aware admission that even Vanna White wouldn't find it funny
jokes.explanation: Johnny's self-deprecating humor about his bad wedding jokes, with a callback to his Wheel of Fortune connections. Shows he knows his material isn't great.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, I've been working on some material for the wedding. I thought I'd incorporate some Wheel of Fortune references.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: But let's be honest, if Vanna White wouldn't laugh at it, then nobody's going to laugh at it.

jokes.text: Even Vanna White didn't laugh at that one.
jokes.setup: Johnny realizing his line didn't work
jokes.punchline: Using Vanna White as the standard for tough audience
jokes.explanation: Clever self-aware humor that extends the game show callback. Vanna as comedy barometer is inspired.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I guess that didn't land. You know, I've bombed in front of tougher crowds than this. I once told a joke to Vanna White and she didn't even turn a letter.

jokes.text: A part of me feels like... I'm almost glad that we lost the money. Well, I'm happy you and your feeling are so well acquainted.
jokes.setup: Alexis expressing genuine emotion about their situation
jokes.punchline: Moira's dismissive comment about Alexis being in touch with her feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira deflection of genuine emotion with a witty putdown that treats feelings like an acquaintance. Shows her discomfort with sentiment.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm actually really upset about this.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, look at you getting in touch with your feelings. How very Oprah of you.

jokes.text: But you wouldn't be the first hostage to fall in love with their captor.
jokes.setup: Alexis expressing fondness for their situation in Schitt's Creek
jokes.punchline: Moira comparing their circumstances to Stockholm Syndrome
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy metaphor - Moira treats their forced stay in town like a kidnapping situation. Perfectly captures her dramatic worldview while being unexpectedly insightful.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm actually really happy here. I love this town, I love the people, and I love our life.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's just Stockholm Syndrome, darling.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, it's not.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Mmm, yes it is. We were taken hostage by circumstance, and now we're identifying with our captors. It's a very common response to prolonged trauma.

jokes.text: - Boop. - Oh.
jokes.setup: Tender mother-daughter moment between Alexis and Moira
jokes.punchline: Alexis booping Moira's nose, Moira's surprised reaction to physical affection
jokes.explanation: Sweet physical comedy moment showing Alexis's playful affection and Moira's awkwardness with physical intimacy. Simple but character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know what? I love you, Mom.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, darling...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Boop.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Did you just... boop my nose?

jokes.text: Are we sure that's not just muscle - Yes. - All right, you answered that too quickly.
jokes.setup: David questioning if his neck tension might just be muscle
jokes.punchline: His paranoia about how quickly the masseur dismissed his theory
jokes.explanation: David's neurotic overthinking of a simple massage interaction. Shows his tendency to find conspiracy in innocent moments.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you're saying it's just muscle tension?

dialogue.speaker: Masseur
dialogue.line: Yes, that's what I'm saying.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You seemed very quick to dismiss that theory.

dialogue.speaker: Masseur
dialogue.line: Because it's correct.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, but you didn't even consider my alternative hypothesis.

jokes.text: Johnny, when I say I know the florist, I mean, I know the florist. - Oh. - Yes! Ronnie! That's great!
jokes.setup: Ronnie offering to get extra flowers through her connections
jokes.punchline: Her emphasis implying a sexual relationship with the florist, Johnny's awkward realization
jokes.explanation: Classic innuendo joke with Ronnie's sexual frankness making Johnny uncomfortable. Straightforward but character-appropriate humor.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: I know the florist. I can get you extra flowers. We go way back.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh. Oh!

jokes.text: I mean, David's gonna have a heart attack, but it... it could work.
jokes.setup: Discussing Town Hall as wedding venue
jokes.punchline: Johnny's accurate assessment of David's likely reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character understanding - Johnny knows exactly how David will react but proceeds anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David will take one look at that place and say 'I'm not getting married in a building that smells like a wet dog that's been dead for three weeks.'

jokes.text: Uh, yeah. That was the first time I've ever received a happy ending. Sorry, what did you just say? The happy ending that the masseur gave me.
jokes.setup: David innocently mentioning his massage experience
jokes.punchline: Sexual misunderstanding about 'happy ending' massage services
jokes.explanation: Classic sexual misunderstanding comedy. While not particularly original, the setup and David's innocence make it work well for the characters.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I went for a massage last week. It was wonderful. The masseuse asked if I wanted the happy ending.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, that's—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I said yes, obviously. And you know what? She brought me a cookie.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: A cookie.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes. It was a very happy ending.

jokes.text: just because I didn't have the Town Hall on my mood board doesn't mean we still can't have fun.
jokes.setup: David accepting the Town Hall venue
jokes.punchline: His reference to having a 'mood board' for wedding venues
jokes.explanation: Perfect David reference - of course he has a mood board for wedding venues. Shows his character even when medicated.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have a mood board.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Of course you do.

jokes.text: He gave you a happy ending?! On our wedding day?!
jokes.setup: The growing misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Patrick's horror at what he believes happened
jokes.explanation: Peak comedy escalation. Patrick's outrage at the perceived betrayal on their wedding day is perfectly played.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Wait, what? No, no, no. You didn't... tell me you didn't actually...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, it's not what you think—

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Oh my God. OH MY GOD. I can't believe you would do this to me. To us. I thought we had something special here!

jokes.text: I thought you wanted that?!
jokes.setup: Patrick's accusatory tone
jokes.punchline: David's confused defense making the misunderstanding worse
jokes.explanation: David's innocent confusion escalates the comedy. His defense makes Patrick think he wanted sexual services.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Are you seriously trying to tell me you didn't know?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Know what? Patrick, I have no idea what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You're going to stand there and lie to my face?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not lying. I genuinely don't know what you mean. Can you please just tell me what this is about?

jokes.text: He gave you a happy ending?! On our wedding day?! I thought you wanted that?! See, this is what Ray should be filming.
jokes.setup: Patrick's shock at the misunderstood massage situation
jokes.punchline: David's confusion and Ray being called out for filming the wrong moments
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misunderstanding with meta-commentary on what should be documented. Ray's presence adds another layer to the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Wait, so you were getting a massage?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, Patrick. A therapeutic massage. For my back.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: And Ray was there?

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: I was documenting it for the business Instagram.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Ray, why would you—

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: I got some great shots of the oils, the ambiance...

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: So what's the problem?

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Well, I may have accidentally focused on David's face the entire time instead of the therapeutic aspects.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Ray, you filmed me looking like I was in pain for forty-five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: It was very dramatic. I thought it was working.

jokes.text: All I did, was leave an envelope full of cash and a note that said to take very good care of you.
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining what he arranged
jokes.punchline: Realizing how his innocent instructions could be misinterpreted
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup that shows how innocent instructions created the misunderstanding. Great structure and payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I arranged for the caterer to come early, set up in the back, and then slip out discreetly once everything was ready.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, that's... that's not what that sounds like.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: What? I just wanted everything to be perfect and out of sight.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, I understand what you *meant*, but telling someone to 'slip out discreetly through the back' is a very different sentence.

jokes.text: This is gonna make for a very strange story one day.
jokes.setup: The entire happy ending misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Patrick's meta commentary on their wedding day story
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor that acknowledges the absurdity of their situation. Provides nice closure to the sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You know, when I think about how we met, and everything we've been through, and now we're here getting married... it's like we're living in a sitcom.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We are living in a sitcom.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: I was both shocked and impressed! Okay, Marcia Clark? Court's adjourned for the day.
jokes.setup: David admitting he was impressed by the supposed sexual service
jokes.punchline: Patrick comparing David to OJ Simpson prosecutor, ending the interrogation
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference comparing Patrick's questioning to a famous prosecutor. Shows Patrick's wit and David's tendency to overshare.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I was impressed.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You were impressed? What are you, the OJ prosecutor? I'm not taking any more questions.

jokes.text: All I did, was leave an envelope full of cash and a note that said to take very good care of you. And now that I've said those words out loud, I can see how a certain kind of person would interpret that the wrong way.
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining his innocent massage booking instructions
jokes.punchline: His dawning realization of how his words could be misinterpreted
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Patrick's innocence and self-awareness. The realization beat works well to resolve the misunderstanding comedically.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I just told them to make sure the masseuse knows to focus on my lower back and... oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I said 'work it out thoroughly' and 'don't be shy about going deep.'

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: That's... that's not what I meant at all.

jokes.text: Something can fall from the ceiling and just go Bffggghhh! I'm dead! Okay. - Not me though, I'm prepared.
jokes.setup: Roland making morbid jokes about Town Hall safety
jokes.punchline: His confidence that he personally would survive any ceiling collapse
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland inappropriate humor with his inexplicable confidence in his own survival skills. The sound effect adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: If this ceiling were to collapse right now, I'd be fine. I'd just walk out of here.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The rest of you, I don't know. But me? I'd be okay.

jokes.text: It's white. You're wearing a white dress. Duh. It's black and white themed. What was I supposed to show up in pink? Or black! You're wearing a wedding dress to my wedding!
jokes.setup: David realizing Alexis is wearing white to his wedding
jokes.punchline: Alexis's oblivious defense and David's horror at the wedding dress situation
jokes.explanation: Classic wedding faux pas comedy with great sibling dynamic. Alexis's fashion obliviousness vs. David's horror creates good tension.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Are you wearing white?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yes, it's a beautiful Valentino gown.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You can't wear white to a wedding. You're not the bride.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, technically, you're the bride.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm the groom, Alexis.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, so it's fine.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, it is not fine. You need to change.

jokes.text: It came with like a white, tulle, headdress, but I thought it overwhelmed the dress so I decided not to wear it. You're walking me down the aisle in a wedding dress.
jokes.setup: Alexis casually mentioning the dress came with a veil
jokes.punchline: David's realization that she's literally wearing a complete wedding ensemble
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - not only is it white, it came with a veil. Alexis's casual mention of rejecting the veil for aesthetic reasons makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: The dress came with a veil.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're wearing an actual wedding dress? With a veil?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, yes, that's what I just said.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, you're literally wearing a complete wedding ensemble to go to the grocery store.

jokes.text: Everyone is gonna think we're getting married to each other.
jokes.setup: David realizing the optics of Alexis in a wedding dress walking him down the aisle
jokes.punchline: His horror at the perceived sibling wedding implication
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy recognizing the awkward visual. David's horror is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. People are going to think we're getting married to each other.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? No, they're not.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, they are. You're in a wedding dress, walking me down the aisle. That's... that's a couple's entrance.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's not a couple's entrance, David. I'm your sister.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, and everyone here knows that, but to a casual observer, this looks deeply inappropriate.

jokes.text: I don't think it's gonna look like a wedding dress unless you guys are standing next to each other. She's walking me down the aisle!
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to minimize the wedding dress problem
jokes.punchline: David pointing out they will literally be standing next to each other
jokes.explanation: Good logical comedy - Stevie's attempt to help backfires when David points out the obvious flaw in her reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It's fine, it's not a big deal. We'll just... we'll keep some distance.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Stevie, we're literally going to be standing next to each other.

jokes.text: - Is that a bow in the back? - Don't, David.
jokes.setup: David discovering even more wedding dress details
jokes.punchline: Stevie cutting him off before he can spiral further
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective escalation of the wedding dress crisis. Stevie's protective intervention shows she knows David well.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And another thing—the seamstress said the train needs to be reinforced because of the weight of the beading, which means we might need to adjust the bustle mechanism, and I'm not even sure if the original design accounted for—

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David. The dress is beautiful. We're not talking about this anymore.

jokes.text: I really wanted to impress you today. And now I feel like I'm ruining your wedding. I think you're giving yourself a lot of credit. My wedding was already ruined.
jokes.setup: Alexis feeling bad about ruining David's wedding
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact statement that it was already ruined before she helped
jokes.explanation: Perfect David Rose deadpan pessimism. His matter-of-fact delivery of devastating news while also being oddly reassuring to Alexis.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I feel terrible about ruining your wedding.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it was already ruined before you helped.

jokes.text: Is it me, or do they look like husband and wife? Kind of.
jokes.setup: David and Alexis walking down the aisle together
jokes.punchline: Wedding guests thinking they're the couple getting married due to Alexis's white dress
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the wedding dress setup. The visual confusion David feared actually happens, validating his concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest 1
dialogue.line: Oh, they look so happy together!

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest 2
dialogue.line: I always knew those two would end up together.

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest 1
dialogue.line: The way they're walking down the aisle... it's so romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest 3
dialogue.line: Wait, I thought David was marrying Patrick?

dialogue.speaker: Wedding Guest 2
dialogue.line: Shh, just enjoy the moment.

jokes.text: Our lives are like little bébé crows, carried upon a curious wind.
jokes.setup: Johnny attempting to give a poetic wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: His bizarre metaphor comparing lives to baby crows
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Johnny attempt at profundity. The specific choice of 'bébé crows' and 'curious wind' creates a memorably weird metaphor.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Love is like a baby crow. It's awkward, it's not very attractive, and you're not entirely sure it's going to make it.

jokes.text: Okay. I think we're good. Okay.
jokes.setup: Johnny's rambling poetic ceremony speech
jokes.punchline: David politely but firmly cutting off his father's officiating
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated interruption. David's polite but firm shutdown of Johnny's rambling shows his need for control while being respectful.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And so as we gather here today, in this moment of profound significance, I find myself reflecting on the nature of love itself, which is much like a river, flowing endlessly through the landscape of our—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad. Dad, we're good. Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: But David, I haven't even gotten to the part about—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, I know. And I appreciate it, but we need to move things along.

jokes.text: Patrick Brewer, you are my happy ending.
jokes.setup: David's wedding vows reaching their emotional climax
jokes.punchline: Callback to the massage misunderstanding earlier in the episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms a sexual innuendo into a beautiful romantic declaration. Perfect economy and emotional payoff that recontextualizes the earlier comedy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And when I think about the future, I think about you, and I think about... actually, I need to be clear about something. Earlier today, when I said I was getting a massage, I meant a therapeutic massage. A professional massage. I was not, in any way, implying that I needed to be... worked on. Manually. By another person. Because I'm with you now, and you're the only person I want to—

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, I know what you meant.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Do you? Because your face suggested otherwise for a solid forty-five minutes.

jokes.text: There's a time and place for sentimentality, and your only son's wedding day is hardly the moment.
jokes.setup: Moira appearing to have held it together at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Her backward logic that a son's wedding is not the time for sentimentality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira logic inversion - treating her son's wedding as an inappropriate time for emotion. Classic character denial and emotional deflection.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A son's wedding is hardly the time to become maudlin. One must remain composed, collected, and entirely emotionally unavailable.

jokes.text: I personally would not have missed this for the whole wide world. Okay. You're only saying that 'cause you haven't gone to bed yet.
jokes.setup: Stevie expressing sentimental feelings about the family leaving
jokes.punchline: David's practical explanation that she's only emotional because she's drunk/tired
jokes.explanation: David deflecting genuine sentiment with practical observations. Shows his discomfort with emotional moments while being somewhat affectionate.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm going to miss you guys. This place, all of it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're drunk.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm not drunk.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're tired then.

jokes.text: Yes, just as sweet as the liqueur wafting off your breath. I also didn't go to bed last night.
jokes.setup: Moira commenting on the alcohol smell from Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie's matter-of-fact admission that she also didn't sleep, just partied
jokes.explanation: Simple humor about post-wedding drinking with Moira's typical vocabulary pretension. Stevie's deadpan response works well.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You smell like a distillery.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I didn't sleep. I just partied.

jokes.text: Make sure the girls are shipped in a... - Temperature controlled - Temperature controlled - cargo container. Aha. - Cargo container. Aha.
jokes.setup: Moira giving instructions about shipping something valuable
jokes.punchline: Referring to her wigs as 'the girls' and treating them like precious cargo
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira wig obsession humor. Her treating the wigs like living beings that need special care perfectly captures her priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Now, when you ship the girls, I want them in individual climate-controlled boxes with their own security detail.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Your wigs need a security detail?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: These aren't just wigs, Johnny. These are the girls. They've been with me through three marriages, two bankruptcies, and a complete loss of cellular service in Schitt's Creek. They deserve to be treated with the utmost care and respect.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'll... make a note of that.

tmdb_overview: David stresses out over the rain on his wedding day, the family scrambles to make it right.
insight: Series finale sustains 85.6 score across 46 jokes with escalation and character beats.
